content.full_text:
F ern P olrrrcer- P RACrrcEs C ouurs s roN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 7,2006
Dear Ms. Hamai:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests provisions of the political Reform Act (th!,,Aci).1
QUESTION
Does the Act prohibit a filing officer/official from completing parts 1 ,2, 3 and the
summary heading on the Statement of Economic Interests (Form zooi) for its filers?
CONCLUSION
No' However, the Act requires that the Form TOObe signed under penalty of
perjury and "verified" by the official who files the form.
Sachi A. Hamai
Executive Officer
Board of Supervisors
County of Los Angeles
383 Kenneth Hahn Hall of Administration
Los Angeles, CA 90012
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-06-195
t Gorernment Code sections g 1000-91014
18109-18991, of the California Code of Regulations
FACTS
Commission regulations appear at title 2, sectioru
The Los Angeles county Board of Supervisors' Executive office presently serves
as the filing officer/official for more than 200 agencies and annually r"""ir", in excess of
2,000 Statements of Economic Interests (Form iool. t, complying with section g1010 it
has been your experience that many filers routinelyi complete ttre g-eneric parts of the
Form 700 incorrectly. The summary portions, whlre one must list their nu-., business
address, office they hold, jurisdiction of that office, and type of statement being filed, are
r.rd4rJ


File No. I-06-195
Page No. 2
areas that the filers often have trouble with, but are also areas that your office ayeady
knows what the correct information should be.
These generic portions of the statements cause the filers problems which, in turn,
require the filing officer/official to spend excessive hours working with the filers to
amend and correct.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that every public official disclose all his or her economic
interests that could foreseeably be affected by the exercise of the official,s duties.
(Sections 81002(c) and 87200.) In addition, pursuant to section g 1004 provides:
"(a) All reports and statements filed under this title shall be signed
under penalty of perjury and verified by the filer. The verification shall
state that the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and
that to the best of his knowredge it is true anJcomplete.
"(b) A report or statement filed by a committee which qualifies
under subdivision (a) of Section 82013 shall be signed and verified by the
treasurer, and a report or statement filed by any oth", p".ron shall be
signed and verified by the firer. If the filer is an entity other than an
individual, the report or statement shall be signed and verifie dby a
responsible officer of the entity or by an attorney or a certifiea puutic
accountant acting as agent for the entity. Every person who signs and
verifies any report or statement required to be hled under this title which
contains material matter which he knows to be false is guilty orp"4rry.,,
Section 81004 does not prohibit another person from completing a statement or
report' However, by signing the verification, the filer is stating that he Ir she has used all
reasonable diligence in preparation of the statement, and to the best of the filer,s


File No. I-06-195
Page No. 3
knowledge, it is true and complete. (Section 81004.) Therefore, the filer should be able
to verify the accuracy of the information on the form submitted.
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
sa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
John W. Wallace
ssistant General Counsel
gal Division

The Act requires that every public official disclose all his or her economic
interests that could foreseeably be affected by the exercise of the official,s duties.
(Sections 81002(c) and 87200.) In addition, pursuant to section g 1004 provides:
"(a) All reports and statements filed under this title shall be signed
under penalty of perjury and verified by the filer. The verification shall
state that the filer has used all reasonable diligence in its preparation, and
that to the best of his knowredge it is true anJcomplete.
"(b) A report or statement filed by a committee which qualifies
under subdivision (a) of Section 82013 shall be signed and verified by the
treasurer, and a report or statement filed by any oth", p".ron shall be
signed and verified by the firer. If the filer is an entity other than an
individual, the report or statement shall be signed and verifie dby a
responsible officer of the entity or by an attorney or a certifiea puutic
accountant acting as agent for the entity. Every person who signs and
verifies any report or statement required to be hled under this title which
contains material matter which he knows to be false is guilty orp"4rry.,,
Section 81004 does not prohibit another person from completing a statement or
report' However, by signing the verification, the filer is stating that he Ir she has used all
